title: API Testing
description: Learn to test REST APIs using Playwright's API testing capabilities

questions.question: Which Playwright object is used for API testing?
questions.explanation: The request fixture/context is used for API testing in Playwright.

questions.question: Complete the code to make a GET request:
questions.explanation: request.get() makes a GET request to the specified URL.

questions.question: Playwright can test REST APIs without opening a browser.
questions.explanation: Playwright's API testing feature works independently of browser automation.

questions.question: How do you send JSON data in a POST request?
questions.explanation: Use the data option to send JSON data in POST requests.

questions.question: Complete the code to get JSON response:
questions.explanation: response.json() parses the response body as JSON.

questions.question: How do you set custom headers for API requests?
questions.explanation: Pass headers in the options object when making requests.

questions.question: What is wrong with this API test?
questions.code:
const response = request.post('https://api.example.com', { data: {} });
await expect(response.status()).toBe(200);
questions.explanation: The request.post() call must be awaited before accessing response properties.

questions.question: API requests in Playwright automatically retry on failure.
questions.explanation: Unlike UI actions, API requests do not have automatic retry by default.

questions.question: Which method checks the API response status?
questions.explanation: response.status() returns the HTTP status code.

questions.question: What does this code validate?
questions.explanation: toBeOK() checks if the response status is in the 200-299 range.
